title: In-Drawer Knife Block
summary: A slotted knife insert that sits inside a kitchen drawer rather than taking up counter space — 2x2 blocks glued to a plywood base, spaced to hold blades, trimmed to fit the drawer.
description:
This is an insert, not a standalone block — it's built to trim-fit inside an existing kitchen drawer, so measure your drawer's interior depth before cutting the plywood base, since the plan's dimensions are a starting point, not a guarantee. Glue and tack the six 2x2 blocks to the plywood back with quick-dry or hot glue first, spacing them evenly to form blade slots between them, then flip the whole assembly over and drive brad nails through the back for a permanent hold.

The 1x2 knife-handle support and the 1x2 divider attach the same way — glue and tack, then nail from the back. **Use shorter 3/4" brad nails for these two pieces**, since they're thinner than the 2x2 blocks and a 1-1/4" nail will blow through the front face.

Once assembled, trim the base to fit snugly inside your actual drawer — a loose insert slides around every time the drawer opens, which defeats the point of building it in the first place.

cutList.note: Can be cut to fit your drawer's depth now instead of trimming later.

steps.title: Position the blocks
steps.body: Use quick-dry or hot glue to position the six 2x2 blocks on the plywood base, evenly spaced to form blade slots between them.

steps.title: Nail from the back
steps.body: Once the glue is dry, flip the assembly over and drive 1-1/4" brad nails through the back into each block.

steps.title: Add the handle support and divider
steps.body: Glue and tack the 1x2 handle support and divider in place the same way, using shorter 3/4" nails since these pieces are thinner than the blocks.

steps.title: Trim to fit
steps.body: Trim the base so it sits snugly inside your drawer and doesn't shift when the drawer opens and closes.

unresolvedImages.alt: In-drawer knife block insert with slotted blocks
